caption.full:
The Television Academy defends The Beast in Me's Emmy nomination.

The Television Academy is defending an Emmy nomination for The Beast in Me's theme music. While Emmy rules state theme music must be at least 15 seconds, the track is reportedly only 13 seconds long. The Academy bases its decisions on official production cue sheets.

Do you think a 13-second theme should count as an eligible Emmy entry?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#TVNews #EmmyAwards #TheBeastInMe #SeanCallery
caption.firstComment: Pin question: If The Beast in Me's cue is only 13 seconds, should it even be in the running, or is it a creative win worth bending the rules for?

youtube.title: Sean Callery's Emmy Nom Defended: 13-Second Theme Music
youtube.description:
Sean Callery's Emmy nomination for The Beast in Me's main title theme music is being defended by The Television Academy. The theme is heard for 13 seconds, under the 15-second eligibility rule. The Academy bases decisions on cue sheets and will refine rules for next year. The show received nine Primetime Emmy nominations.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

Watch more: https://thescreenreport.com/celebrity/tv-academy-defends-beast-in-me-emmy-nomination-amid-eligibility-questions/
